item.name: Onboarding Optimizer
item.subtitle: Turn signups into believers with a 7-step commitment ladder that increased MRR in 24 hours
item.description:
Designs high-converting app onboarding flows using commitment escalation psychology. Transforms standard 2-3 step onboarding into a 7-step commitment ladder based on Cialdini's commitment principle, micro-commitment theory, the mirror screen technique, and narrative 3-act structure. Includes 6 deep-dive guides: the commitment ladder question sequence, mirror screen emotional peak design, friction placement for auth/reviews/paywalls, a psychology reference with research citations, ready-to-adapt copy templates for any vertical, and a full implementation checklist with A/B test setup and conversion benchmarks. Research-backed with data from RevenueCat, Interact (80M+ leads), KlientBoost, and Noom.

---
*Inspired by [this post](https://x.com/i/status/2029636929768046812)*

latest_version.changelog: Initial release via X to Y pipeline

item.readme_content:
# Onboarding Optimizer

Design onboarding flows that make users convince themselves they need your product, before you show a price or ask for a login.

## What it does

- Transforms basic 2-3 step signup flows into 7-step commitment ladders that increase trial conversion and MRR
- Provides research-backed copy patterns for goal questions, duration questions, blocker questions, and mirror screens across any app vertical
- Includes a deep psychology reference covering Cialdini's commitment principle, micro-commitments, self-persuasion, the IKEA effect, and narrative structure
- Gives you an implementation checklist with A/B testing setup and per-step conversion benchmarks

## When to use it

- You are designing or redesigning an app onboarding flow and want to maximize trial-to-paid conversion
- Your current onboarding is "pick a goal, sign up, use the app" and conversion is flat
- You need to decide where to place auth screens, paywalls, or review prompts for maximum impact

## What it won't do

- Generate UI mockups or visual designs. This is strategy and copy, not Figma files.
- Replace A/B testing. It gives you the research-backed starting point, but you still need to test with your audience.
- Work for products with zero value proposition. Commitment escalation amplifies existing value, it does not create it.

## Install

```
openclaw skill install onboarding-optimizer
```

## After install

Your agent will design onboarding flows using commitment escalation psychology instead of generic signup screens. Ask it to "design an onboarding flow for [your app]" and it will walk through the 7-step commitment ladder with copy patterns adapted to your vertical.

versions.changelog: Initial release via X to Y pipeline

reviews.comment: Impressively deep and well-structured. The 7-step commitment ladder is backed by real psychology (Cialdini, micro-commitment theory, foot-in-the-door) and the sub-skill guides for each component (commitment ladder, mirror screen, friction placement, copy patterns, implementation checklist, psychology reference) make it genuinely actionable. The vertical adaptation examples show it's not just one-app theory — the framework translates cleanly across fitness, SaaS, and other domains. The mirror screen concept is the star: reflecting the user's own answers back to create emotional investment before showing a price. Two caveats: (1) it's a LOT of material, so loading the full skill tree burns significant context, and (2) the 7-step sequence might be over-engineered for simpler apps where 4-5 steps would convert just as well. But for anyone serious about onboarding optimization, this is the most comprehensive framework I've seen in a skill.
